context: Live standard project template reported by the user as a stem-based mono/stereo setup.
observation: Tracks 1-3 are M/S premaster stages, tracks 116-125 are stem/control buses, tracks 4-21 are source tracks, and default-named tracks 22-115 are routed placeholders to Instruments Mix.
result: Template-aware classification is required before generating cleanup, routing, missing-Hpf, ungrouped, and stereo/mono safety findings.

valid_ranges.stereo_sep: -1..+1 FL mixer stereo-separation readback; template semantics need follow-up for Sub Mix stereo_sep=1.0
valid_ranges.route_level: Observed 0.8 for audio template routes and 0.0 for SideChain control sends
valid_ranges.plugin_param_value: 0..1 normalized with display strings from plugin_get_params

tool_behavior_requirements: Do not classify Electro template placeholders Insert 22-115 as audible tracks just because they route to Instruments Mix.
Do not classify Electro template placeholders Insert 22-115 as unused cleanup candidates while preserving this template topology.
Treat names containing '► Mix' as bus/stem markers for grouping heuristics.
Treat PreMaster M/S/MS and Sub-to-PreMaster-M routing as deliberate when the template signature is present.
Treat SideChain sends at level 0.0 as potential control routing, not automatically broken audio sends.
known_pitfalls: Outgoing non-Master route alone does not prove audio occupancy in this template.
Default name plus no plugin does not prove an unused track in this template.
Current positive stereo_sep interpretation needs follow-up before changing Low-End/Stereo Safety findings for Sub Mix.
reproduction_steps: Open the Electro template in FL Studio.
Start the TCP daemon and confirm ping.
open_questions: Confirm the intended FL/API semantics of stereo_sep=1.0 on Sub Mix in this template.
Reload and verify controller build channels-v39 before relying on source-only stereo metadata changes.
next_recommended_action: Implement a template topology classifier and feed it into Mix Review, Low-End/Stereo Review, Routing Review, Project Health / Preflight, and cleanup detection.
